note: Project-level rollup. MVP COMPLETE: ORCH, M0 (10 repos), M0.5, M1–M9 all DONE and CI-green on the A.5 matrix. M9: new graphed-preserve repo — the analysis preservation bundle. build_bundle captures the canonical IR (M8, opt_level=0) + M6 provenance/sourcemap + every External payload (real correctionlib JSON + ONNX model) by content hash in the M8 Store + input datasets + environment + config + seed into a manifest whose hash is the bundle fingerprint; reproduce interprets the IR (awkward backend + payload-backed evaluators) bit-for-bit on a clean machine with no originals (raising UnresolvedPayload on any missing reference); inspect renders IR + provenance + payload inventory + opaque-node risk flags without executing (faithful to reproduce). Self-fingerprinting (changes iff a content/result input changes). graphed-core gained GraphStore.nodes() IR introspection; graphed gained Session.sourcemap(). All 10 package repos public. M10 REMEDIATION (2026-06-09): the mvp-shortcomings.md findings fixed across the repos — genuine IncrementalReducer + Session(incremental=True) (A.1); IR-driven execution via compile_ir/evaluate_ir, one dispatch per REDUCED node, fork executor glue rewired (A.2); buffer-level projection project_buffers/necessary_buffers, count-only -> {col: OFFSETS} served from TTree counter branches (A.3); full A.5 CI matrices + build-only wheels (NO PyPI publish) + cargo llvm-cov gate, guarded by scripts/test_workflows.py (B.4/B.5); opt-in maximal stage fusion + full symmetric commute rule set + ParamMap token-injectivity fix (C.6/C.7); opt-in pooled combines (C.8); first-class Partition.blind, fork sentinel retired under freeze-UPROOT-1, graphed_write hardening (C.9); integrity scan catches bare-pass targets (C.10). Every fix has a NEW frozen suite (tests/frozen/m10); no pre-existing frozen test modified except the recorded freeze-UPROOT-1 encoding amendment.

ORCH.title: Deterministic orchestrator engine (Part B + B.8 faker suite)
ORCH.evidence: 41 frozen tests, 97% line+branch coverage, mypy --strict clean, CI green on A.5 matrix incl. 3.14/3.14t [2026-06-05] Hardened: added a CI-confirmation gate (GateReport.ci/done_ready) so a milestone can never be recorded DONE off an unfinished CI run (in_progress counts as not-green); review() holds in REVIEW with an 'awaiting_ci' signal until the EXACT pinned commit's CI is verified. New ci.py + scripts/confirm_ci.py (pure-Python, replaces shell gh-watch loops and the zsh word-split bug). 54 frozen tests; re-validated via the new confirm-on-pinned-commit flow.

M0.title: Repository spine (per repo)
M0.note: DONE per-repo across all 10 package repos (latest: graphed-preserve M0 spine)

M0.5.title: Reference corpus study & Required Operations Catalog
M0.5.evidence: ops_catalog + 23 runnable fixtures (ADL 1-8, AGC ttbar 2x5, TTGamma) reproducing stored references bit-for-bit on the full OS matrix; 60 frozen tests; graph-bloat note. Synthetic data + ONNX fixture flagged Phase-2.

M1.title: graphed-core thread-safe interned graph IR
M1.evidence: interning by structural hash; External PayloadDescriptor in hash; total-order float hashing (NaN self-interns, 0.0/-0.0 distinct); single-Mutex Send+Sync verified by loom + multi-thread stress; PyO3 gil_used=false. 21 frozen Python tests + 6 cargo tests + loom; CI green on A.5 matrix incl. free-threaded 3.14/3.14t. No unsafe.

M2.title: frontend + backend protocol + graphed-numpy
M2.evidence: deferred Array proxy records backend-agnostic IR into graphed-core; one node+form per op, repeated sub-exprs intern to zero nodes; backend-independence proven (two toy backends, identical to_dot, different results); map->External with preservation-risk descriptor; provenance stub raises ill-typed ops at the user's source line. graphed 12 tests/94%, graphed-numpy 14 tests/100%. CI green on A.5 matrix.

M3.title: graphed-awkward typetracer forms + provenance
M3.evidence: AwkwardBackend op_form via awkward typetracer (metadata-only, no data read) + real eval via one shared dispatch; gak namespace mirrors the awkward API. ADL queries 1-8 + AGC object-selection slice all record metadata-only with correct forms; materialize matches plain awkward; every node maps to a user line. Real provenance (graphed) with sub-expression text via executing, toggle, helper/comprehension survival. correctionlib + ONNX External nodes content-hash the file. graphed 24 tests/91%, graphed-awkward 32 tests/98%. CI green on A.5 matrix. ADL 1-8 run end-to-end as integration tests reproducing corpus histograms bit-for-bit (caught + fixed a q8 port bug). graphed-awkward 41 tests/97%.

M4.title: graphed-core optimizer: DCE/CSE + equality-saturation stage fusion
M4.evidence: RewriteEngine trait (egg-free boundary; no egg leak) + EggEngine genuine equality saturation with SOUND rules only (commutativity, +/* identities; mask-fusion/field-collapse excluded as unsound). DCE+CSE outside the engine; stage fusion never crosses a boundary. O(N) cost-quotient extraction (egg's Extractor is O(N^2) on deep chains — caught + guarded by the super-linear benchmark). 10k-node systematics graph reduces to O(stage) in <1s; reduction sub-linear across {1k,2k,4k,8k}; byte-stable. 13 Rust tests (toy-interpreter semantic equivalence) + 13 M4 Python tests; clippy/rustfmt/mypy/ruff/sphinx-W/loom clean. CI green on A.5 matrix.

M5.title: Necessary-buffer (column) projection
M5.evidence: Necessary-buffer (column) projection. graphed: Session.walk + Projection/OnFail/handle_opaque. graphed-awkward: reporting typetracer (metadata-only) -> touched form-keys -> columns; dimuon reads only the muon branches + MET.pt (5 of 20). graphed-numpy: NON-trivial projection via record sources + field-touch tracking (per user request). OVER-TOUCH PROTECTED in both (per user request): reading one column reads only that column; jets[jets.pt>30].eta reads exactly {Jet.pt,Jet.eta}, never siblings (the dask-awkward bug). Opaque map honors pass|warn|raise. Projection store-state independent (correct after M4 fusion). graphed 32, graphed-numpy 32, graphed-awkward 55 tests. CI green.

M6.title: graphed-debug: opt-level lowering, source-mapped tracebacks, viz
M6.evidence: graphed-debug (new repo). lower(session,array,opt_level): 0 = 1:1 op<->node + inter-op assertions; >=1 fuses op-runs between boundaries, each op keeping exact provenance (same error line at any opt level). StageError carries failing op + user source-frame chain + input forms + partition, all plain data; custom __reduce__/__setstate__ make it PICKLABLE -> round-trips intact across a process boundary (never an opaque string). run() executes op-by-op, localizing failures deep inside a fused stage. format_traceback collapses graphed* frames (user line last/marked), identical after pickle. visualize() = deterministic Mermaid/Graphviz + provenance + projected columns. 29 frozen tests incl. a REAL multiprocessing (spawn) process-boundary test re-raising at the user's analysis line (A.3 #8); 97% coverage; green on the full A.5 matrix incl. 3.14t. (graphed gained Session.source_value support.)

M7.title: Execution-layer contract + graphed-exec-local
M7.evidence: Execution layer. graphed-core owns the contract (Executor/Plan/Task/Partition/StopCondition, data-only). graphed-exec-local: BOTH a ThreadExecutor and a ProcessExecutor (spawn) running a Plan to one result via a deterministic, straggler-tolerant tree reduction (fixed combine-tree by leaf key -> bit-for-bit; a slow leaf blocks only its own path). open_once file-locality (per worker), stopping conditions, adaptive reshaping (next_tasks + running fold), remote StageError surfaced intact via M6 (process mode). 33 frozen tests: tree reduction, both executors thread==process, thousands of tiny tasks no deadlock/stall/race (incl 3.14t), straggler doesn't block others, open_once once-per-worker, adaptive + target-events stop, remote StageError, and a real MET analysis over partitions through BOTH executors reproducing the single-pass histogram BIT-FOR-BIT invariant to partition count. graphed-core M7 contract: 8 tests. CI green on A.5 matrix incl. 3.14t.

M8.title: graphed-checkpoint: plan serialization + checkpoint/resume
M8.deployment_pattern: compile once, run on N datasets: graphed-core adds DurablePlan.with_partitions/for_dataset/for_datasets + Dataset/partition_dataset/partition_datasets (the optimized interned IR is reused unchanged across datasets; per-dataset content-addressed task_ids namespace one Store); graphed adds Session.serialized_ir(*outputs, optimize=True) as the 'compile' step (record -> M4-optimize -> byte-identical IR). graphed-checkpoint integration test compiles a realistic multi-observable analysis once (71-node graph -> 23 interned stage-nodes) and runs it over N synthetic datasets sharing one Store: per-dataset bit-for-bit, kill-mid-deployment + resume skips completed datasets/chunks, chunk-size invariant, cheap retargeting. CI-green on all three repos.
M8.evidence: graphed-core: GraphStore.serialize/deserialize = canonical versioned byte-identical IR (GIR1; round-trips incl. Stage nodes); DurablePlan wraps the IR + executor metadata (partitions/read_columns/reduction/stopping/locality/resource), versioned byte-identical to_bytes, content-addressed task_id (SHA-256 over IR + process spec + partition -> cache-poisoning-safe); OpSpec resolves callables by import-ref or, only for opaque callables, embeds by value (cloudpickle) flagged opaque=True; 21 frozen tests incl. runs-with-no-source-files; 97% cov. graphed-checkpoint (new repo): content-addressed Store (atomic temp+fsync+rename blobs, append-only journal tolerant of torn lines + missing-blob entries, append-only dead-letter); run_resumable skips completed task_ids and recombines per-task partials in deterministic order -> kill+resume equals an uninterrupted run BIT-FOR-BIT with no double-count/no lost partition and measurably less work; retry_n/retry_smaller_chunk (recovers a chunk too big to process whole)/retry_elsewhere/quarantine; error budget stop; dead-letter harvests M6 StageError provenance; serialized plan runs in a source-free subprocess; kill/resume on a real ADL-q1 MET analysis bit-for-bit. 26 frozen tests, 96% cov. ruff + mypy --strict + sphinx -W + cargo clippy/fmt clean; CI green on the A.5 matrix incl. 3.14/3.14t.

M9.title: graphed-preserve: analysis preservation bundle
M9.externals_plugin_refactor: Externals generalized into a validated plugin system (no longer hardwired to onnx/correctionlib). An ExternalPlugin gives a kind a deterministic content-based content_hash (ONNX -> hash of weights + graph structure; correctionlib -> hash of canonical contents; sha256_bytes -> raw bytes), an evaluate, and >=2 sample payloads. register_plugin VALIDATES the hash before trusting it: cross-process determinism (runs the hash under two PYTHONHASHSEEDs via a cloudpickle-by-value subprocess, rejecting any hash built from hash()/id()/time/randomness) and non-vacuity (distinct payloads must not collide -> rejects a constant/trivial hash). record_external(session, plugin, payload, inputs) records a preservable External via graphed-awkward's new generic 'external' op; the same plugin.evaluate runs at build-time materialize and at reproduce -> bit-for-bit. build_bundle verifies each stored payload hashes to its recorded id (cache-poisoning-safe). onnx + correctionlib ship as plugins and double as templates; a user-defined plugin is tested end-to-end (record -> preserve -> reproduce bit-for-bit, fingerprint tracks the payload). graphed-awkward gained op='external' (additive; correction/onnx/map unchanged, M3 frozen intact). The plugin API was stress-tested against real ML frameworks (PyTorch TorchScript w/ weights-hash + multi-input, XGBoost booster bytes, and the NVIDIA Triton remote-inference pattern) — optional [mltest] extra, importorskip so CI skips them; the shape held. Findings then addressed: a per-worker ResourceCache (load(payload,params)->resource + close) so a model/connection is materialized once (open_once for Externals), and the remote/connection hook makes Triton first-class. A dedicated CI 'triton' job starts a real nvcr.io/nvidia/tritonserver container serving tests/samples/triton_models and reproduces a bundle through it over BOTH gRPC and HTTP, bit-for-bit vs a numpy reference (env-gated; skipped locally). torch+xgboost vendor clashing OpenMP runtimes (env capture matters).
M9.evidence: graphed-preserve (new repo, the final milestone). build_bundle(root, *, session, value, weight, datasets, payloads, histogram, config, seed) captures: the canonical serialized IR (M8, opt_level=0 so it is auditable + 1:1 with user ops); the M6 provenance/sourcemap (basenames -> build-location independent); every External PayloadDescriptor + the real correctionlib JSON + ONNX model bytes (in the M8 content-addressed Store); input datasets by content hash; environment (python + pinned versions, optional container digest); config + seed; into a canonical manifest whose SHA-256 is the bundle fingerprint. reproduce(bundle) interprets the IR (op/reduction via the awkward backend; External via payload-backed correctionlib/onnx evaluators that are the SAME code as build-time -> bit-for-bit; sources from the Store) and raises UnresolvedPayload('... <hash>') on any missing reference (never a silent wrong result). inspect(bundle) renders IR + provenance + payload inventory + opaque-node risk flags WITHOUT executing, faithful to reproduce (same GraphStore.nodes() IR). Self-fingerprinting: the fingerprint changes iff a content/result input changes (correctionlib JSON, ONNX weights, dataset, config, seed, env) and NOT on irrelevant ones (no timestamps/abs-paths; provenance basenames). AGC-ttbar fixture: reduced ttbar slice recorded through graphed/graphed-awkward with a real correctionlib v2 SF (weight systematic), a real ONNX model (per-event inference), and JES (kinematic) + correctionlib (weight) systematics. 21 frozen tests incl. a machine-B scrubbed-subprocess reproduce (originals deleted, agc unimportable) matching bit-for-bit; 95% cov. graphed-core gained GraphStore.nodes() IR introspection (5 tests); graphed gained Session.sourcemap() (1 test). ruff + mypy --strict + sphinx -W clean; CI green on the full A.5 matrix (3 OS x py3.11-3.13 + 3.14/3.14t experimental). Resolved M9 design choices: all payloads referenced by hash from the Store (never inlined); environment captured (not enforced -> container is Phase-2); opt_level=0 IR; one systematic variation per bundle (systematics-as-axis is Phase-2).

M11.title: dask.array parity P0: full ufunc tier + backend-idiom factorization (array_type; NumpyArray proxy; awkward stays functions-only)
M11.note: Re-frozen as freeze-M11-1 after a human-directed design review: graphed.Array stays backend-idiom-neutral; the original numpy-flavored freeze-M11-0 commits were backed out to backup/m11-m12-numpy-flavored before any push.

M13.title: dask.array parity P2: indexing (slices/ints common; tuple subscripts numpy-side) + manipulation with axis-0 geometry rules + concatenate/unique/bincount/histogram*

M14.title: dask.array parity P3.8: graphed.apply multi-input blockwise externals + signature-aware apply_gufunc (P3.9 + P4 deferred to Phase 2 by user decision)

M15.title: Partitioned parquet I/O: backend-agnostic base in graphed (blind partitions, deferred write plans) + awkward specialization (schema-only forms, buffer-projection-wired writes) + numpy rectilinear specialization (R16.7 amended by user decision: parquet alone enters the numpy MVP)

M16.title: dask-awkward parity P0: full canonical ufunc tier on the awkward backend, the structural reduction rule on every reducer (name-based boundary classification retired), 13 new reducers

M17.title: dask-awkward parity P1: ~20 structure functions through the single apply dispatch + the common record-subset getitem (a[[fields]])

M18.title: dask-awkward parity P2: behaviors — with_name + backend behavior registration; vector four-vectors record, evaluate, and project to exactly the leaves they read

M19.title: dask-awkward parity P3.8: fields/type_of/backend_of introspection (recording nothing), head/sample peeking, common slice/index ops on the awkward backend; __awkward_function__ dispatch and the rest stay Phase 2 by user decision

M37.title: Live execution dashboard (FINOS Perspective + websockets + a network transport): passive Monitor seam (graphed-core, unchanged), executor emit (graphed-exec-local), and graphed-debug's DashboardServer (perspective Server + Tornado) + NetworkMonitor (websocket, loopback or remote) + Dashboard + pyinstrument profile rows. Determinism gate green attached-or-not. Root prompt R20. (SSE/uPlot prototype on m37-sse-uplot.)
